data.title: Enriched task descriptions: Downstream Consumers section
data.description:
## User Story
As an implementation-plan user, when a task modifies files with external consumers, I want a `## Downstream Consumers` section in the generated task description so sub-agents know which other modules depend on the files they're changing.

## Done Definitions
1. When generating a task description, implementation-plan detects external consumers for each file in the task's file impact list using ast-grep (preferred) or fuzzy-match fallback
2. If >=1 consumer detected for any file, a `## Downstream Consumers` section is added, listing each consumer with its file path
3. Section is structurally distinct from `## File Impact` (separate heading, not nested)
4. When no consumers found for any file, the section is omitted entirely (never an empty section)
5. When ast-grep unavailable, fuzzy-match fallback used; when both fail, section omitted with log warning
6. Tests: structural boundary test validates `## Downstream Consumers` heading format in task description; integration test: given a file with a known importer, task description contains the importer in the Downstream Consumers section

## Considerations
- [Performance] ast-grep/fuzzy-match on task files adds latency; consider limiting consumer search depth

## Escalation Policy
Escalate when blocked: Agents proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. All assumptions made without escalating are documented.
